description: Access Graph (Identity Security) view — users, roles, resources, and security alerts backed by live SQL queries against the CNPG Postgres database.

panels.description:
Total Teleport users known to Access Graph.

**SQL:**
```sql
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT count(*) FROM nodes WHERE kind='identity' AND subkind='user'
```

targets.rawSql:
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT count(*) FROM nodes WHERE kind='identity' AND subkind='user'

panels.title: Users

panels.description:
Total Teleport roles known to Access Graph.

**SQL:**
```sql
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT count(*) FROM nodes WHERE kind='identity_group' AND subkind='role'
```

targets.rawSql:
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT count(*) FROM nodes WHERE kind='identity_group' AND subkind='role'

panels.title: Roles

panels.description:
Total registered Teleport resources (SSH nodes, kube clusters, apps, databases, desktops).

**SQL:**
```sql
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT count(*) FROM nodes WHERE kind='resource'
```

targets.rawSql:
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT count(*) FROM nodes WHERE kind='resource'

panels.title: Resources

panels.description:
Open security alerts detected by Access Graph. Any value above 0 warrants investigation.

**SQL:**
```sql
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT count(*) FROM security_alerts WHERE status='OPEN'
```

targets.rawSql:
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT count(*) FROM security_alerts WHERE status='OPEN'

panels.title: Active Security Alerts

panels.description:
Users with a standing_privileges score > 0. Standing access is always-on access that doesn't require an access request.

**SQL:**
```sql
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT count(*) FROM nodes WHERE kind='identity' AND subkind='user' AND (value->'properties'->>'standing_privileges')::int > 0
```

targets.rawSql:
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT count(*) FROM nodes WHERE kind='identity' AND subkind='user' AND (value->'properties'->>'standing_privileges')::int > 0

panels.title: Users w/ Standing Privileges

panels.description:
Users whose weakest MFA device is UNSET or empty — no MFA enrolled.

**SQL:**
```sql
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT count(*) FROM nodes WHERE kind='identity' AND subkind='user' AND value->'properties'->>'weakest_mfa_device_kind' IN ('UNSET','')
```

targets.rawSql:
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT count(*) FROM nodes WHERE kind='identity' AND subkind='user' AND value->'properties'->>'weakest_mfa_device_kind' IN ('UNSET','')

panels.title: Users w/o MFA

panels.description:
Count of roles with no users, no bot impersonations, and no JIT escalation path leading to them — candidates for cleanup. Aggregates by role NAME to handle Access Graph occasional duplicate node records for the same role.

**SQL:**
```sql
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT count(*) FROM (
  SELECT DISTINCT r.value->>'name' FROM nodes r
  WHERE r.kind='identity_group' AND r.subkind='role'
    AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM nodes r2
      JOIN edges e ON e.to_node = r2.id
      WHERE r2.kind='identity_group' AND r2.subkind='role'
        AND r2.value->>'name' = r.value->>'name'
        AND e.kind IN ('member_of','impersonator_of','requester_of')
    )
) sub
```

targets.rawSql:
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT count(*) FROM (
  SELECT DISTINCT r.value->>'name' FROM nodes r
  WHERE r.kind='identity_group' AND r.subkind='role'
    AND NOT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM nodes r2
      JOIN edges e ON e.to_node = r2.id
      WHERE r2.kind='identity_group' AND r2.subkind='role'
        AND r2.value->>'name' = r.value->>'name'
        AND e.kind IN ('member_of','impersonator_of','requester_of')
    )
) sub

panels.title: Dead Roles

panels.description:
Bot identities that hold impersonation rights over at least one Teleport role.

**SQL:**
```sql
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT count(DISTINCT from_node) FROM edges WHERE kind='impersonator_of'
```

targets.rawSql:
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT count(DISTINCT from_node) FROM edges WHERE kind='impersonator_of'

panels.title: Bots w/ Impersonation Rights

panels.description:
All users with their standing privilege score, role count, MFA type, crown jewel flag, and lock status. Higher standing score = more sensitive account.

**SQL:**
```sql
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT
  value->>'name'                                  AS user,
  value->'properties'->>'standing_privileges'     AS standing,
  value->'properties'->>'identity_groups'         AS roles_count,
  value->'properties'->>'weakest_mfa_device_kind' AS mfa,
  value->'properties'->>'is_crown_jewel'          AS crown_jewel,
  value->'login_status'->>'is_locked'             AS locked
FROM nodes WHERE kind='identity' AND subkind='user'
ORDER BY (value->'properties'->>'standing_privileges')::int DESC
```

targets.rawSql:
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT
  value->>'name'                                  AS user,
  value->'properties'->>'standing_privileges'     AS standing,
  value->'properties'->>'identity_groups'         AS roles_count,
  value->'properties'->>'weakest_mfa_device_kind' AS mfa,
  value->'properties'->>'is_crown_jewel'          AS crown_jewel,
  value->'login_status'->>'is_locked'             AS locked
FROM nodes WHERE kind='identity' AND subkind='user'
ORDER BY (value->'properties'->>'standing_privileges')::int DESC

panels.title: Users — Standing Privileges & MFA

panels.description:
Every user's direct SSH logins through their standing role memberships — who can SSH where, and as which UNIX user, without needing an access request.

**SQL:**
```sql
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT DISTINCT
  u.value->>'name' AS user,
  r.value->>'name' AS role,
  a.value->'properties'->'teleport'->'ssh'->'logins'->'original_values' AS logins
FROM edges m
JOIN nodes u ON u.id=m.from_node AND u.kind='identity'
JOIN nodes r ON r.id=m.to_node   AND r.kind='identity_group'
JOIN edges ra ON ra.from_node=r.id AND ra.kind='access'
JOIN nodes a ON a.id=ra.to_node   AND a.kind='action' AND a.subkind='ssh'
WHERE m.kind='member_of'
```

targets.rawSql:
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT DISTINCT
  u.value->>'name' AS user,
  r.value->>'name' AS role,
  a.value->'properties'->'teleport'->'ssh'->'logins'->'original_values' AS logins
FROM edges m
JOIN nodes u ON u.id=m.from_node AND u.kind='identity'
JOIN nodes r ON r.id=m.to_node   AND r.kind='identity_group'
JOIN edges ra ON ra.from_node=r.id AND ra.kind='access'
JOIN nodes a ON a.id=ra.to_node   AND a.kind='action' AND a.subkind='ssh'
WHERE m.kind='member_of'

panels.title: Standing SSH Access

panels.description:
Bot identities and the Teleport roles they can impersonate. Bot impersonation grants the full permission set of the target role to the machine identity.

**SQL:**
```sql
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT u.value->>'name' AS bot, r.value->>'name' AS impersonates_role
FROM edges e
JOIN nodes u ON u.id=e.from_node
JOIN nodes r ON r.id=e.to_node
WHERE e.kind='impersonator_of'
```

targets.rawSql:
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT u.value->>'name' AS bot, r.value->>'name' AS impersonates_role
FROM edges e
JOIN nodes u ON u.id=e.from_node
JOIN nodes r ON r.id=e.to_node
WHERE e.kind='impersonator_of'

panels.title: Bot Impersonations

panels.description:
Which roles can submit access requests for which other (elevated) roles. Maps the JIT escalation topology.

**SQL:**
```sql
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT r1.value->>'name' AS base_role, r2.value->>'name' AS can_request
FROM edges e
JOIN nodes r1 ON r1.id=e.from_node
JOIN nodes r2 ON r2.id=e.to_node
WHERE e.kind='requester_of'
  AND r1.value->>'name' NOT IN ('requester','okta-requester')
ORDER BY base_role, can_request
```

targets.rawSql:
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT r1.value->>'name' AS base_role, r2.value->>'name' AS can_request
FROM edges e
JOIN nodes r1 ON r1.id=e.from_node
JOIN nodes r2 ON r2.id=e.to_node
WHERE e.kind='requester_of'
  AND r1.value->>'name' NOT IN ('requester','okta-requester')
ORDER BY base_role, can_request

panels.title: Access Request Escalation Paths

panels.description:
For each requestable role, the set of reviewer roles that can approve it. Roles with no reviewers are un-approvable.

**SQL:**
```sql
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT r2.value->>'name' AS target_role,
       string_agg(DISTINCT r1.value->>'name', ', ' ORDER BY r1.value->>'name') AS reviewers
FROM edges e
JOIN nodes r1 ON r1.id=e.from_node
JOIN nodes r2 ON r2.id=e.to_node
WHERE e.kind='reviewer_of' AND r2.value->>'name' LIKE 'role-%'
GROUP BY target_role
ORDER BY target_role
```

targets.rawSql:
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT r2.value->>'name' AS target_role,
       string_agg(DISTINCT r1.value->>'name', ', ' ORDER BY r1.value->>'name') AS reviewers
FROM edges e
JOIN nodes r1 ON r1.id=e.from_node
JOIN nodes r2 ON r2.id=e.to_node
WHERE e.kind='reviewer_of' AND r2.value->>'name' LIKE 'role-%'
GROUP BY target_role
ORDER BY target_role

panels.title: Reviewers Per Role

panels.description:
Full inventory of every Teleport resource by type. Use labels to verify expected coverage or spot unlabelled resources.

**SQL:**
```sql
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT subkind AS kind, value->>'name' AS name, value->'labels' AS labels
FROM nodes WHERE kind='resource' ORDER BY subkind, name
```
panels.title: Resource Inventory

panels.description:
Top 25 roles by the number of distinct resource groups they grant access to. High counts indicate large blast radius if the role's accounts are compromised.

**SQL:**
```sql
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT r.value->>'name' AS role, count(DISTINCT e2.to_node) AS resource_groups_granted
FROM nodes r
JOIN edges e1 ON e1.from_node=r.id AND e1.kind='access'
JOIN nodes a  ON a.id=e1.to_node   AND a.kind='action'
JOIN edges e2 ON e2.from_node=a.id AND e2.kind='access'
WHERE r.kind='identity_group' AND r.subkind='role'
GROUP BY role
ORDER BY resource_groups_granted DESC
LIMIT 25
```

targets.rawSql:
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT r.value->>'name' AS role, count(DISTINCT e2.to_node) AS resource_groups_granted
FROM nodes r
JOIN edges e1 ON e1.from_node=r.id AND e1.kind='access'
JOIN nodes a  ON a.id=e1.to_node   AND a.kind='action'
JOIN edges e2 ON e2.from_node=a.id AND e2.kind='access'
WHERE r.kind='identity_group' AND r.subkind='role'
GROUP BY role
ORDER BY resource_groups_granted DESC
LIMIT 25

panels.title: Blast Radius (Top 25 Roles)

panels.description:
Roles granting access to the wildcard resource group {"*":"*"} — these bypass all label-based segmentation and have the highest effective blast radius.

**SQL:**
```sql
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT DISTINCT r.value->>'name' AS role, a.subkind AS resource_kind
FROM edges e1
JOIN nodes r  ON r.id=e1.from_node AND r.kind='identity_group'
JOIN nodes a  ON a.id=e1.to_node   AND a.kind='action'
JOIN edges e2 ON e2.from_node=a.id
JOIN nodes rg ON rg.id=e2.to_node  AND rg.kind='resource_group'
WHERE rg.value->>'name' = '{"*":"*"}'
ORDER BY role, resource_kind
```

targets.rawSql:
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT DISTINCT r.value->>'name' AS role, a.subkind AS resource_kind
FROM edges e1
JOIN nodes r  ON r.id=e1.from_node AND r.kind='identity_group'
JOIN nodes a  ON a.id=e1.to_node   AND a.kind='action'
JOIN edges e2 ON e2.from_node=a.id
JOIN nodes rg ON rg.id=e2.to_node  AND rg.kind='resource_group'
WHERE rg.value->>'name' = '{"*":"*"}'
ORDER BY role, resource_kind

panels.title: Wildcard-Access Roles

panels.description:
Roles requiring MFA re-authentication or hardware trusted-device verification at the action level. Cross-reference with Wildcard-Access Roles to ensure the most powerful roles have adequate assurance requirements.

**SQL:**
```sql
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT DISTINCT value->>'name' AS role,
       value->'properties'->'teleport'->>'require_mfa_type' AS mfa,
       value->'properties'->'teleport'->>'trusted_device'   AS trusted_device
FROM nodes
WHERE kind='action'
  AND (value->'properties'->'teleport'->>'require_mfa_type' <> 'OFF'
       OR value->'properties'->'teleport'->>'trusted_device' = 'required')
ORDER BY role
```

targets.rawSql:
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT DISTINCT value->>'name' AS role,
       value->'properties'->'teleport'->>'require_mfa_type' AS mfa,
       value->'properties'->'teleport'->>'trusted_device'   AS trusted_device
FROM nodes
WHERE kind='action'
  AND (value->'properties'->'teleport'->>'require_mfa_type' <> 'OFF'
       OR value->'properties'->'teleport'->>'trusted_device' = 'required')
ORDER BY role

panels.title: MFA-Required Roles

panels.description:
Roles with no users, no bot impersonations, and no JIT escalation path leading to them — candidates for cleanup. Aggregates by role NAME to handle Access Graph occasional duplicate node records for the same role.

**SQL:**
```sql
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT DISTINCT r.value->>'name' AS role,
       r.value->'properties'->'teleport'->>'description' AS description
FROM nodes r
WHERE r.kind='identity_group' AND r.subkind='role'
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM nodes r2
    JOIN edges e ON e.to_node = r2.id
    WHERE r2.kind='identity_group' AND r2.subkind='role'
      AND r2.value->>'name' = r.value->>'name'
      AND e.kind IN ('member_of','impersonator_of','requester_of')
  )
ORDER BY role
```

targets.rawSql:
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT DISTINCT r.value->>'name' AS role,
       r.value->'properties'->'teleport'->>'description' AS description
FROM nodes r
WHERE r.kind='identity_group' AND r.subkind='role'
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM nodes r2
    JOIN edges e ON e.to_node = r2.id
    WHERE r2.kind='identity_group' AND r2.subkind='role'
      AND r2.value->>'name' = r.value->>'name'
      AND e.kind IN ('member_of','impersonator_of','requester_of')
  )
ORDER BY role

panels.title: Dead Roles

panels.description:
All security alerts from Access Graph, most recent first. The data column contains the full structured finding. Filter by severity or status to focus on critical open issues.

**SQL:**
```sql
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT severity, status, kind, data->>'created_at' AS created_at, data
FROM security_alerts
ORDER BY (data->>'created_at') DESC
LIMIT 50
```

targets.rawSql:
SET search_path TO ${tenant};
SELECT severity, status, kind, data->>'created_at' AS created_at, data
FROM security_alerts
ORDER BY (data->>'created_at') DESC
LIMIT 50

panels.title: Active Security Alerts

list.definition: SELECT schema_name FROM information_schema.schemata WHERE schema_name LIKE 'tenant_%' ORDER BY 1;
list.description: Tenant schema inside the access_graph database. There is typically one per Teleport cluster.
list.name: tenant
list.query: SELECT schema_name FROM information_schema.schemata WHERE schema_name LIKE 'tenant_%' ORDER BY 1;

title: Teleport Identity Security
